name: dependency-upgrade
description: Manage major dependency version upgrades with compatibility analysis, staged rollout, and comprehensive testing. Use when upgrading framework versions, updating major dependencies, or managing breaking changes in libraries.
qualityReason: Clear scope, focused on major upgrades
capabilities: Analyze dependency trees to identify outdated packages
Plan staged upgrade strategies for major version bumps
Generate compatibility matrices for framework upgrades
Configure automated dependency updates with Renovate or Dependabot
Execute rollback plans if upgrades fail
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and guidance for dependency upgrades. The static scanner flagged external commands, network access, and filesystem operations, but these are all FALSE POSITIVES: the 'external commands' are example snippets in markdown code blocks, the single network call (line 176) is a legitimate curl to fetch public GitHub documentation, and filesystem operations are example code in documentation. No executable code is present - this is a pure guidance/metadata skill.
valueStatement: Managing major dependency upgrades is complex and error-prone. This skill provides structured guidance for compatibility analysis, staged rollouts, and comprehensive testing to minimize breaking changes.
limitations: Does not execute actual package manager commands - only provides guidance
Cannot automatically fix code that depends on deprecated APIs
Requires human verification of test results
Compatibility matrices are examples and may need customization

useCases.title: Upgrade React from v16 to v18
useCases.description: Plan and execute a safe React 16 to 18 upgrade with staged testing and compatibility checks

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers dealing with dependency hell
useCases.title: Resolve npm dependency conflicts
useCases.description: Audit project dependencies and resolve peer dependency conflicts using npm 7+ overrides

useCases.title: Set up automated dependency updates
useCases.description: Configure Renovate or Dependabot to automatically manage minor and patch updates

promptTemplates.title: Basic dependency audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the dependency-upgrade skill to audit my project's dependencies. I want to know which packages are outdated and which have security vulnerabilities.
promptTemplates.scenario: A developer wants a quick overview of their project's dependency status

promptTemplates.title: Plan major version upgrade
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to upgrade React from version 16 to version 18 in my project. Use the dependency-upgrade skill to create a staged upgrade plan with testing checkpoints.
promptTemplates.scenario: A frontend developer needs guidance for a major framework upgrade

promptTemplates.title: Resolve peer dependency conflicts
promptTemplates.prompt: My npm install is failing due to peer dependency conflicts. Help me identify the conflicts and provide solutions using npm overrides or legacy-peer-deps.
promptTemplates.scenario: A developer troubleshooting npm install failures

promptTemplates.title: Set up automated updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure automated dependency updates for my Node.js project using Renovate or Dependabot. I want minor and patch updates to auto-merge but major updates to require review.
promptTemplates.scenario: A team lead wanting to automate dependency maintenance

bestPractices: Always read changelogs before upgrading major versions to understand breaking changes
Upgrade one major version at a time (e.g., 16->17->18, not 16->18)
Run the full test suite after each individual package upgrade
antiPatterns: Upgrading all dependencies at once instead of incrementally
Skipping peer dependency warnings without resolving them
Not having a rollback plan before starting major upgrades

faq.question: Can this skill automatically upgrade my dependencies?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance, planning, and best practices. It does not execute package manager commands. You must run the suggested commands yourself and verify results.

faq.question: How do I handle peer dependency conflicts?
faq.answer: Use npm 8+ overrides in package.json, or try npm install --legacy-peer-deps for npm 7. For long-term solutions, update conflicting packages to compatible versions.

faq.question: What is the safest way to upgrade a major version?
faq.answer: Create a backup branch, upgrade one major version at a time, run tests after each upgrade, and fix issues before proceeding to the next version.

faq.question: Should I use yarn or npm for dependency upgrades?
faq.answer: Both work well. npm has better peer dependency handling in v7+. yarn has better deduplication. Choose based on your team's preference and lock file consistency.

faq.question: How do I automate dependency updates?
faq.answer: Configure Renovate or Dependabot in your repository. Set automerge for minor/patch updates and require reviews for major updates.

faq.question: What if an upgrade breaks my application?
faq.answer: Have a rollback plan: commit current state, create feature branch, test incrementally. If tests fail, checkout the previous branch and restore from lock file.
